name: Artichoke Paste
recipeIngredient: 2 tablespoons finely diced onion or shallots
3 sprigs of thyme, or a couple of pinches of dried thyme
1 small branch of tarragon or 1/4 teaspoon dried tarragon
Fresh lemon juice or tarragon vinegar to taste
Chopped chervil or tarragon for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim the artichokes down to their hearts (they can be quartered) and set them in a bowl of water acidulated with the lemon juice as you work.
2. Use a pan large enough to hold all the artichokes in a single layer.
3. Add water just to cover, then add the lemon peel, onion, garlic, bay leaf, thyme, tarragon, and parsley.
4. Bring to a boil, then lower the heat and simmer, adding small amounts of water as needed, until the artichokes are tender and just a few tablespoons of liquid remain.
5. Remove the bay leaf, lemon, parsley, and thyme branches from the pan.
6. Transfer the artichokes to a food processor and puree, adding the remaining pan juices as needed to thin the mixture.
7. If any fibers from the artichokes remain, pass the puree through a food mill.
8. Season to taste with salt and pepper.
9. Add a little lemon juice or vinegar to sharpen the flavors (again, do this to taste - the puree may already be fine as it is).
10. Heap into a bowl and garnish with the chopped chervil or tarragon.
Note: Artichokes make a subtle and delicate spread to serve with crackers. The best time to make this is when large spring artichokes from California are available and inexpensive. If available, garnish the spread with chopped chervil or tarragon.
Serving size: 1/4 cup
Calories: 54
Fat: 0.3 gram
Cholesterol: 0 mg
Sodium: 175 mg (without added salt)
